conflicts.description: Claude's demolition job is described as an airport-side motel job in chapter_03 and an old medical building off Twenty-Seventh in chapter_09, but chapter_10 stages Delroy's injury at Palm Court Apartments, an old fourplex off Seventy-First.
conflicts.canonical_value: Palm Court Apartments, an old fourplex off Seventy-First.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 10's site layout drives the injury mechanics and aftermath, so it is the hardest version to change and becomes canon.

conflicts.description: Chapter_13 names remittance receipts as 60, 80, 45, and 100, while chapter_14 lays out five receipts totaling 270 dollars: 45, 60, 50, 40, and 75.
conflicts.canonical_value: The visible receipts total $270 across five transfers: $45, $60, $50, $40, and $75.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 14's confrontation depends on Marcia reading out each receipt and totaling them in-scene, so that accounting is more causally embedded than Tiana's earlier rough read.

conflicts.description: The previous continuity sheet merged Marcia's nursing-school admission with Delroy's remittance receipts and implied those facts were hidden from Marcia and Delroy; the manuscript shows Tiana learns them in chapter_13, but Marcia already knew about the nursing letter and Delroy already knew about the remittances.
conflicts.canonical_value: Keep Delroy's remittances as his hidden pre-story secret; treat Marcia's nursing-school admission as something Tiana discovers in chapter_13, not a fact hidden from Marcia herself.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 13 explicitly shows Tiana finding both documents and Marcia later confirming the nursing-school admission, so the old combined knowledge-state entry had to be split and corrected.
